llm_result.summary: The case shows rapid cycling between elevated activation (initiating many projects, increased talkativeness, impulsive spending) followed by crashes with fatigue and social withdrawal, disrupting routines. Modifiable predictors span bio (sleep/energy regulation), psycho (activation planning, impulse control, goal pursuit), and social/environmental factors (boundaries, accountability). Proposing seven targeted, actionable levers across BIO/PSYCHO/SOCIAL domains (and their interactions) with simple metrics enables tracking and iterative optimization to reduce cycle severity, improve follow-through, and stabilize daily functioning.

solutions.predictor_variable: Sleep regularization and circadian alignment (consistent bed/wake times, light exposure, wind-down routine)
solutions.solution_candidate: Implement a fixed daily sleep-wake schedule with a structured wind-down, morning light exposure, and screens-off window 1 hour before bed. Use a wearable/app to monitor sleep duration and onset/offset consistency; adjust bedtime gradually to stabilize daytime energy and reduce activation spikes.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Regular sleep supports neuroenergetics, mood stability, and executive functioning, reducing abrupt shifts in energy/activation and easing social engagement rhythms.
solutions.measurement: Sleep duration and bed/wake time consistency (hours, SD); daily energy/rating (0-10); number of elevated-activation episodes per week; daytime functioning index
solutions.mapping_query: S01 maps to C04/C05/C06; measure sleep logs and energy patterns

solutions.predictor_variable: Activation management through time-blocking and precommitment (limit initiation, one-in/one-out rule)
solutions.solution_candidate: Adopt daily planning with calendar blocks that cap new project initiation (e.g., 1–2 new projects per day). Implement a rule to pause initiation unless a concrete plan is formed. Track adherence and outcome.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Constraining initiation reduces fragmentation, lowers cognitive load during elevated states, and promotes deliberate activation and sustainable follow-through.
solutions.measurement: Number of new projects started per week; proportion of initiated tasks that reach completion; adherence rate to time-blocking rules
solutions.mapping_query: S02 maps to C01; measurement via initiation rate

solutions.solution_candidate: Set a weekly discretionary budget with automatic transfers to savings. Enable alert-based controls and friction (e.g., require multiple confirmations for large purchases). Eliminate spontaneous purchases beyond budget.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Environmental and cognitive friction reduces impulsive spending during elevated moods, preserving resources and reducing regret that disrupts routines.
solutions.measurement: Discretionary purchases ratio; weekly spend vs. budget; number of unplanned purchases avoided
solutions.mapping_query: S03 maps to C03; measurement via budget logs

solutions.solution_candidate: Establish dedicated communication windows (e.g., 3 blocks per day) and a Do Not Disturb period to protect recovery after elevated episodes. Use planned social interactions and assertive communication to manage talkativeness and withdrawal tendencies.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Structured boundaries prevent overload during high-activation periods and allow energy recovery, reducing withdrawal severity and preserving social functioning.
solutions.measurement: Response latency and frequency in messaging; daily talk time; mood stability after episodes; perceived social connectedness
solutions.mapping_query: S04 maps to C02 and C05; measurement via communication logs and mood diaries

solutions.predictor_variable: Routine anchoring and energy restoration (morning/evening routines; energy-aware planning)
solutions.solution_candidate: Create two fixed anchor routines (morning and evening) with simple, scalable steps. Use habit stacking to add tasks gradually. Track adherence and energy changes.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Stable routines provide predictability, regulate arousal, and support social engagement, reducing exhaustion and disruption.
solutions.measurement: Routine adherence rate; morning energy level; days with routine disruption
solutions.mapping_query: S05 maps to C06; measurement via routine logs

solutions.predictor_variable: Implementation intentions and progress monitoring (SMART plans, weekly reviews, checklists)
solutions.solution_candidate: Develop explicit if-then plans for follow-through on initiated projects. Schedule weekly progress reviews with a accountability partner; maintain task checklists and feedback loops.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Explicit plans and regular review reduce ambiguity, increase commitment, and boost completion rates during volatile activation cycles.
solutions.measurement: Task completion rate; time to completion; proportion of planned steps completed weekly
solutions.mapping_query: S06 maps to C07; measurement via completion metrics

solutions.predictor_variable: External accountability and social support network
solutions.solution_candidate: Engage a coach or accountability partner; set up weekly check-ins; share goals with a trusted network to sustain commitment and provide feedback during cycles.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Social accountability distributes cognitive load, stabilizes activation patterns, and supports follow-through across cycles by providing feedback and motivation.
solutions.measurement: Number of check-ins completed; adherence to commitments; mood stability across cycles; project completion rate
solutions.mapping_query: S07 maps to C01/C04/C05/C07; measurement via accountability logs and progress reports
